commit_messages: 425ab4b: chore: remove old Wiz branding artifacts
f9fa45a: [Claude] Add metrics, benchmarks, tests, and demo
d9362a1: [Claude] Add ARCHITECTURE.md and navigability annotations to all modules
ebee58f: [Claude] Update README — fix module tree, stats, repo URL

doji_scan.note: Doji scanned 0 files — suspected config issue after fixer/ subpackage refactor. Needs investigation.

findings.title: _current_session is thread-unsafe global mutated from worker threads
findings.detail: _current_session is a module-level mutable global with no synchronization. analyzer.py dispatches file analysis across a ThreadPoolExecutor. Each worker calls session.files_scanned += 1 which is a non-atomic read-modify-write. With 8 workers, files_scanned will silently lose increments under contention.
findings.evidence: detector.py:800: session.files_scanned += 1 called from ThreadPoolExecutor workers. metrics.py:88: _current_session: Optional[SessionMetrics] = None — no lock.
findings.suggestion: Use a thread-safe queue for metric events and reduce at end_session(), or add a threading.Lock to SessionMetrics.

findings.title: FixRuleMetrics is dead code — defined, tested, used nowhere
findings.detail: FixRuleMetrics is a dataclass with avg_duration_ms and success_rate properties. Never instantiated outside test_metrics.py. record_fix() uses a plain dict, not FixRuleMetrics. Tests pass but prove nothing about real behavior.
findings.evidence: grep -r FixRuleMetrics returns hits only in metrics.py:27 (definition) and test_metrics.py (tests). record_fix() at line 72 creates a plain dict.
findings.suggestion: Wire FixRuleMetrics into record_fix() or delete both the class and its tests.

findings.title: MCP tools accept arbitrary paths — no boundary validation
findings.detail: All five MCP tools take a path parameter from the MCP caller, resolve it with Path(path).resolve(), then read/scan the file. No check that the resolved path falls within an allowed directory. An MCP client can read any file on the filesystem. doji_explain returns full structural breakdowns.
findings.evidence: mcp_server.py:52 (_read_file): filepath = Path(path).resolve() — no boundary check, proceeds to read and return content.
findings.suggestion: Add an allowlist/root constraint. Consider a mcp_allowed_roots config option in .doji.toml.

findings.title: Demo docstring still says 'Wiz' — old branding missed
findings.detail: Commit 425ab4b is titled 'remove old Wiz branding' but demo.py line 1 still says 'issues for Wiz to find and fix'.
findings.evidence: demo.py:1: Demo script with intentional issues for Wiz to find and fix.
findings.suggestion: Change to 'Dojigiri'.

findings.title: os.environ used without importing os — demo crashes on execution
findings.detail: demo.py imports only json but uses os.environ on lines 5-6. Running python demo.py raises NameError before reaching any interesting bugs.
findings.evidence: Line 3: import json. Line 5: API_KEY = os.environ['API_KEY'] — os never imported.
findings.suggestion: Add import os or document that the file is intentionally non-runnable.

findings.title: Session lifecycle tests mutate module-level global without cleanup
findings.detail: start_session() sets _current_session. If test fails between start and end, every subsequent test sees stale state. No autouse fixture to guarantee cleanup.
findings.evidence: Line 82: session = start_session() — sets global. No finally or fixture.
findings.suggestion: Add autouse fixture: @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) def clean_session(): yield; end_session()

findings.title: eval-usage fixer excluded from idempotency and round-trip property tests
findings.detail: SAFE_DETERMINISTIC_FIXERS lists 4 rules but omits eval-usage. eval fixer tested separately for parse preservation only, not idempotency (Invariant 2) or detection round-trip (Invariant 5).
findings.evidence: SAFE_DETERMINISTIC_FIXERS at line 231 does not include eval-usage.
findings.suggestion: Add eval-usage to both SAFE_DETERMINISTIC_FIXERS and ROUNDTRIP_FIXERS, or document why it's excluded.

findings.title: load_history uses naive datetimes — breaks across DST transitions
findings.detail: started_at uses datetime.now().isoformat() (naive local time). load_history subtracts naive datetimes. DST changes and timezone shifts make historical comparisons unreliable.
findings.evidence: Line 94: datetime.now().isoformat(). Line 133-134: (datetime.now() - session_dt).days — naive subtraction.
findings.suggestion: Use datetime.now(timezone.utc) throughout.

findings.title: clone_or_update silently swallows git clone/pull failures
findings.detail: subprocess.run() calls use default check=False and capture_output=True, discarding errors. A failed clone/pull returns the path as if it succeeded.
findings.evidence: Line 56-57: subprocess.run(..., capture_output=True, timeout=60) — no check=True.
findings.suggestion: Add check=True to clone. Check .returncode and warn on pull failures.

findings.title: sys.path.insert(0, ...) is fragile import manipulation
findings.detail: Modifies import path to make dojigiri importable. Breaks if run from different directory, if dojigiri is installed as package, or if structure changes.
findings.evidence: Line 25: sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent))
findings.suggestion: Use python -m invocation or add guard: try: import dojigiri except ImportError: sys.path.insert(...).

findings.title: save_session filename collision on rapid successive scans
findings.detail: Filename uses second-resolution timestamp. Two scans starting in the same second silently overwrite each other.
findings.evidence: Line 94: timespec='seconds'. Line 115: path = METRICS_DIR / f'{ts}.json'.
findings.suggestion: Use microsecond resolution or append a random suffix.

findings.title: test_load_history never tests the exclusion branch of date filtering
findings.detail: Test creates sessions all within 5 days of now and loads with days=30. The filter-out path is never exercised.
findings.evidence: All test sessions are within 30 days. No test where days=1 excludes older sessions.
findings.suggestion: Add a test with days=2 where one session is 5 days old — verify it's excluded.

findings.title: Property tests use mkstemp instead of pytest tmp_path — silent cleanup failures
findings.detail: tempfile.mkstemp() with manual cleanup in try/finally. Silent OSError catch means leaked temp files over 200+ hypothesis iterations.
findings.evidence: Lines 405-412: try: os.unlink(tmp_path) except OSError: pass.
findings.suggestion: Use pytest tmp_path fixture for automatic cleanup.

findings.title: File count claims (35 modules) now stale after fixer subpackage split
findings.detail: fixer/ subpackage adds 5 modules. ARCHITECTURE.md and README both say 35. Actual count is ~40.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:200: '35 Python modules'. fixer/ now has 6 files.
findings.suggestion: Update counts or remove them from prose — static counts are a maintenance tax.

findings.title: Badge claims '1122 passed' — hardcoded, not from CI
findings.detail: Static badge URL with hardcoded count. Shows 1122 forever regardless of actual test count. For a code quality tool, a fake green badge is reputationally hazardous.
findings.evidence: Line 9: static badge URL.
findings.suggestion: Link to CI badge or remove until CI is set up.

findings.title: Navigability docstring 'Called by' list is already incomplete
findings.detail: metrics.py says 'Called by: llm.py, detector.py, fixer.py' but is also called by __main__.py, fixer/engine.py, fixer/helpers.py.
findings.evidence: metrics.py:6 vs actual grep results.
findings.suggestion: Expand the list or link to ARCHITECTURE.md as single source of truth.

findings.title: Taint fixture claims html.escape sanitizes eval() — semantically wrong
findings.detail: html.escape prevents XSS but does nothing for eval() code execution. html.escape("__import__('os').system('rm -rf /')") returns unchanged. If the taint engine uses this as a test case, it learns a false negative.
findings.evidence: Line 11-13: sanitized = html.escape(user_input) then eval(sanitized) with comment 'This should be safe'.
findings.suggestion: Use a sink where html.escape is actually valid (innerHTML), or use ast.literal_eval as the sanitized version.

findings.title: Git clone could execute hooks from cloned repos
findings.detail: clone_or_update runs git clone on hardcoded URLs. shell=True is correctly NOT used. Risk limited to: if REPOS dict is modified to add malicious URL, git hooks in cloned repo execute.
findings.evidence: subprocess.run(['git', 'clone', ...]) — no hook protection.
findings.suggestion: Add --config core.hooksPath=/dev/null to git clone for defense-in-depth.

findings.title: --no-clone flag parsed but never used
findings.detail: args.no_clone is parsed but never checked. clone_or_update always runs. User expecting offline mode is misled.
findings.evidence: args.no_clone appears nowhere after argparse declaration.
findings.suggestion: Wire up the flag or remove it.

findings.title: Metrics filename from timestamp — safe today, fragile if externalized
findings.detail: save_session builds filename from started_at. Currently set internally by datetime.now(). If ever set from external input, path traversal possible.
findings.evidence: ts = metrics.started_at.replace(':', '-')
findings.suggestion: No action needed now. Sanitize if SessionMetrics becomes externally constructable.

findings.title: Intentional vulnerabilities confirmed as test fixture
findings.detail: demo.py contains SQL injection, eval(input()), os.environ credentials. Docstring confirms intentional. Not importable by production code.
findings.evidence: Line 1: 'Demo script with intentional issues'.
findings.suggestion: No action needed.

verdict.summary: Thread-unsafe metrics and dead FixRuleMetrics class are the top issues. MCP path traversal is the security finding. New metrics module needs more robust threading, datetime handling, and test coverage.
